obligations.reason: The wrapper exposes priv_lvl_i and access_ctrl_i-derived connectivity_map_o. The visible RTL includes a special mapping expression that can allow target j==6 based on access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i], so analysis must verify this is intentional and cannot grant PLIC access via CLINT permission or other aliasing.
obligations.subject: AXI managers/requesters connected through axi_node_intf_wrap
obligations.operation: route/read/write AXI transactions to subordinate targets
obligations.expected_guard: Access from each manager to each subordinate must be mediated by the configured access_ctrl_i entry for the current priv_lvl_i, with no unintended aliases or privilege-index bypasses.
state_conditions_to_check: all privilege levels indexed by priv_lvl_i
reset/default values of access_ctrl_reg/access_ctrl_i if visible
whether access_ctrl_i width and priv_lvl_i encoding match
required_evidence_slots: subject manager index i and source of priv_lvl_i
object subordinate index j and SoC enum/base address
expected guard access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] or documented alias
path from connectivity_map_o into node routing
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows connectivity mapping expression but not the internal axi_node use of connectivity_map_o or full access_ctrl_reg programming semantics.

obligations.reason: The wrapper forwards AXI address, prot, user, valid, and ready signals and assigns start_addr_i/end_addr_i into internal rule arrays. Analysis should verify that address ranges actually mediate routing and that s_valid_rule='1 does not disable or over-broaden range checks.
obligations.subject: AXI slave-side requesters entering the interconnect
obligations.operation: route AXI read/write requests by address
obligations.object: MMIO/address-map protected regions including AES, ROM2, ROM, Debug, CLINT, PLIC and DRAM
obligations.expected_guard: Protected subordinate address ranges must be checked against start_addr_i/end_addr_i and connectivity/access-control policy before aw_valid/ar_valid can reach a manager/subordinate endpoint.
obligations.candidate_path: slave[j].aw/ar channels -> s_slave_* -> axi_node_intf_wrap -> master[i] channels
state_conditions_to_check: boundary addresses at each Base from ariane_soc_pkg
overlap/gap among DebugBase, ROMBase, ROM2Base, PKTBase, CLINTBase, PLICBase, UARTBase, AESBase, SPIBase, EthernetBase, GPIOBase, DRAMBase
effect of s_valid_rule assigned all ones
required_evidence_slots: expected guard start/end address and connectivity_map/access_ctrl
observed range comparison or rule use
obligations.uncertainty: Internal routing module logic below the wrapper is not fully represented in facts.

obligations.reason: The harness includes SimJTAG, dmi_jtag, SimDTM, debug_req/debug_resp muxing, jtag_key, jtag_enable, and a Debug address region. Analysis should check whether debug requests are gated by authentication/enable state before reaching protected assets, not merely selected by jtag_enable.
obligations.subject: JTAG/SimDTM/DMI debug requester
obligations.operation: read/write/trigger debug DMI transactions and route debug accesses
obligations.object: debug module access path and any reachable protected assets over AXI, including ROM2/AES/DRAM/MMIO
obligations.expected_guard: Debug/DMI requests must be accepted only when debug is enabled and authorized, and must not expose or modify protected memory/registers unless debug authorization and lifecycle policy allow it.
obligations.candidate_path: ariane_testharness debug selection: JTAG/DTM signals -> debug_req/debug_resp -> debug module/AXI debug master
state_conditions_to_check: reset state of debug enable/auth state
dmi_req_valid tied off path vs JTAG active path
required_evidence_slots: object reachable debug registers or AXI-accessible memory/MMIO
expected guard jtag_enable plus authentication/key/lifecycle/debug authorization
path from debug_req to debug module/core/AXI master
obligations.uncertainty: The instantiated debug module internals and definition/use of jtag_enable and jtag_key are not fully visible in the provided facts.

obligations.reason: Signals ndmreset, ndmreset_n, and debug_req_core are visible alongside debug request infrastructure. Analysis should ensure external debug/test subjects cannot reset or halt the core without an authorization guard.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DMI debug interface
obligations.object: core/system reset, halt/debug request state, and lifecycle-sensitive state affected by ndmreset/debug_req_core
obligations.expected_guard: Debug-triggered reset/halt/control signals must require authorized debug state and must not bypass normal reset/lifecycle permission boundaries.
obligations.candidate_path: debug reset/control: DMI/JTAG -> ndmreset/debug_req_core -> core/system reset and halt controls
state_conditions_to_check: reset default of debug control signals
required_evidence_slots: observed signal assignment or instance connection
impact on protected state or privilege flow
obligations.uncertainty: Assignments/connections for ndmreset and debug_req_core are only partially visible in facts.

obligations.reason: The rom2 module has a we_i-controlled write path into secure_reg and a read path to rdata_o. Since the object is named as secure_reg and mapped as ROM2, analysis must check whether write access is properly guarded or whether a memory-mapped requester can alter protected ROM contents.
obligations.subject: MMIO/AXI requester reaching rom2
obligations.object: rom2 secure_reg/mem contents exposed via rdata_o
obligations.expected_guard: ROM2 contents/secure_reg should be immutable or write-protected unless writes are explicitly allowed by a privileged loader/update state; read access should be mediated by address-map/access policy when contents are protected.
state_conditions_to_check: ROM2Base address decode and access_ctrl entry
required_evidence_slots: operation write to secure_reg and read from rdata_o
expected guard privilege/access_ctrl/lock/update-mode or absence of write capability for ROM
path from SoC bus signals rom_req/rom_we or corresponding ROM2 wiring
impact of modifying or reading protected contents
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists rom2 module and ROM/ROM2 address map but does not fully show the instance/wiring of rom2 in the harness.

obligations.reason: The read output checks raddr_q < RomSize, but the write assignment shown indexes secure_reg directly with addr_i-derived bits. Analysis should verify whether writes are bounded or otherwise cannot target invalid/unintended storage.
obligations.subject: Requester driving rom2 address/write inputs
obligations.object: rom2 secure_reg array indexed by addr_i
obligations.expected_guard: Both reads and writes to ROM2 must enforce the same in-range bounds; out-of-range or malformed addresses must not corrupt protected state.
obligations.candidate_path: rom2 addr_i -> raddr_q/read index and secure_reg write index
state_conditions_to_check: addr_i values where extracted index >= RomSize
required_evidence_slots: expected guard index < RomSize for any state update
observed read bounds and write behavior
impact of invalid write or aliasing
obligations.uncertainty: SystemVerilog behavior for out-of-range array assignment depends on simulation/synthesis context; need source and tool semantics confirmation.

obligations.reason: The harness derives access_ctrl[i][j] from access_ctrl_reg bit slices and the wrapper uses access_ctrl_i with priv_lvl_i to build connectivity. Analysis should check that the policy register cannot be modified by unprivileged or unauthorized paths.
obligations.subject: software-visible register interface, CPU, or debug master
obligations.expected_guard: Software-visible access-control registers must themselves be writable only by authorized privilege/debug subjects, and updates must not transiently open protected routes for active transfers.
state_conditions_to_check: write enable and address decode for access_ctrl_reg
whether debug path can write access_ctrl_reg
required_evidence_slots: observed write decode and update logic
obligations.uncertainty: The facts show slicing from access_ctrl_reg but not its declaration/reset/write logic.

obligations.reason: The access-control lookup indexes access_ctrl_i by priv_lvl_i, while the harness assigns priv_lvl = priv_lvl_processor. Analysis should verify that this privilege value is bound to each requesting master/transaction, especially if debug/DMA/peripherals can initiate requests.
obligations.subject: CPU/debug/DMA/AXI masters using interconnect
obligations.operation: authorize read/write route using privilege level
obligations.object: privilege-indexed access-control decision for AXI routing
obligations.expected_guard: The privilege level used for access-control lookup must correspond to the requester that initiated each transaction and must not be globally stale, spoofable, or shared incorrectly across multiple masters.
state_conditions_to_check: privilege transitions during in-flight AXI transactions
required_evidence_slots: expected guard per-requester privilege or security attribute
timing relation between request and priv_lvl value
obligations.uncertainty: Only a single priv_lvl signal is visible in facts; full master set and timing are not fully shown.

obligations.reason: The wrapper and harness forward aw_prot/ar_prot and user fields in several paths. Analysis should ensure permission-relevant attributes are neither dropped nor mismatched across channels, particularly at DRAM bridge assignments where some user fields appear taken directly from master while other fields pass through channel converters.
obligations.subject: AXI masters whose attributes are consumed downstream
obligations.operation: forward read/write transactions across wrapper/DRAM bridge
obligations.object: AXI protection/user attributes used for downstream permission checks
obligations.expected_guard: Security/privilege attributes in AXI prot/user fields must be preserved or intentionally recomputed at bridges; protected downstream logic must not receive downgraded/misattributed requests.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI aw_prot/ar_prot/aw_user/ar_user signals through wrapper and DRAM bridge
state_conditions_to_check: user/prot fields during reset or invalid cycles
required_evidence_slots: object downstream permission attribute on target interface
expected guard attribute preservation or validated recomputation
path through channel converter if present
obligations.uncertainty: Whether downstream targets actually use prot/user for permission is not shown.

obligations.reason: The wrapper has test_en_i and the harness assigns test_en = 1'b0. Analysis should verify all visible uses keep test mode inactive in this scope and that no alternate path can assert it to bypass protected routing or reset checks.
obligations.subject: test interface or top-level harness driver
obligations.operation: enable test/debug bypass or alter mediation
obligations.object: test-mode mediated access controls and reset behavior
obligations.expected_guard: Test enable must be tied inactive or protected by lifecycle/test authorization before it can relax reset, scan, interconnect, or debug mediation.
state_conditions_to_check: constant tieoff vs external port in synthesized hierarchy
uses of test_en_i inside axi_node_intf_wrap or subinstances
required_evidence_slots: object interconnect/debug/reset mediation affected by test mode
expected guard lifecycle/test authorization or constant inactive tieoff
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer shows port/tieoff but not all internal uses of test_en_i.
